goal: Expose the existing Builder orchestrator chain through a thin POST /probe/dry-run endpoint that forces dryRun:true and skipDeploy:true so Phase A can be triggered without a Render shell.
scope_out: Do not modify orchestrator, pipeline, scope resolver, gate client, provider, smart-push, patch-mode, executor, or GitHub bridge logic.
Do not add an endpoint that can perform a real write, push, deploy, or kill-switch change.
Do not allow request body values to override dryRun:true or skipDeploy:true.
Do not log, return, or echo MAYA_CORE_GATE_TOKEN or MAYA_BUILDER_GATE_TOKEN values.
Do not change dependencies, package files, tools, soulmatch, or maya-core.
claims: POST /probe/dry-run parses JSON body { instruction: string } and rejects missing or non-string instruction with 400 before calling the orchestrator.
The handler calls orchestrateTask with dryRun:true and skipDeploy:true as server-side literals; request body dryRun, skipDeploy, approval, or deploy fields are ignored.
The response summarizes OpusTaskResult fields including status, runId, summary, scopeFiles, and a gate summary with reachable, reason, pushAllowed, and requiredExternalApproval.
GET /probe/dry-run returns 405; unknown routes still fall through to the existing health handler behavior.
The response contains no token or secret value.
assumptions: orchestrateTask already chains scope resolution, worker phases, Maya gate, safety policy, and dry-run behavior internally.
BP-136 made maya-core budget, corridor, and cost gates reachable from the deployed Bluepilot Builder.
Phase A intentionally proves the chain with writes still closed; opening the write kill-switch is a separate future task.
dod: POST /probe/dry-run with { instruction } returns 200 and JSON with status, runId, scopeFiles, and gate summary fields when the orchestrator returns a dry-run result.
GET /probe/dry-run returns 405 and missing instruction returns 400 without calling orchestrateTask.
Tests prove the call to orchestrateTask receives dryRun:true and skipDeploy:true even when the request body tries to set dryRun:false or skipDeploy:false.
git grep for MAYA_CORE_GATE_TOKEN and MAYA_BUILDER_GATE_TOKEN finds no occurrence in builder/src/probeDryRun.ts.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
stop_conditions: Implementing the endpoint would require modifying orchestrator, gate, provider, or write-path logic.
A request body value would need to control dryRun or skipDeploy.
A secret value would need to be logged, returned, or committed.
A dependency or package-lock change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BP-135 added /health/maya-gate. BP-136 fixed maya-core middleware so the gate token reaches budget, corridor, cost, and memory routes. Live probe shows the gate is reachable and corridor blocks writes with kill_switch_closed, which is the expected Phase-A safety state.
